summary: Dependency-confusion proof-of-concept squatting an internal package name
details: Package self-describes in its README as a proof-of-concept demonstrating a dependency-confusion vulnerability against an unclaimed internal package name referenced in a public Walmart repository. The package.json description field reads 'walmart Application and Middleware Server'. The published tarball contains only README.md and package.json — there is no main entry, no bin, no lifecycle scripts, and no executable code. Installing this version has no install-time or import-time effect on the installer beyond resolving the name. The risk is that an organization whose internal tooling references this name without a private-registry scope could resolve to this public package instead, and a future version under the same name could ship a payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
